message.content:
You are implementing Task 6 of an auth implementation plan for a NestJS + Fastify backend.

## Context
- Working directory: /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend
- NestJS 11 + Fastify, strict TypeScript
- Read backend/CLAUDE.md for ALL conventions
- Tabs for indentation (useTabs: true in .prettierrc)
- printWidth: 90

## Convention Rules (CRITICAL)
- NO comments in code
- NO relative imports â€” use @aliases only
- ALL functions must have explicit return types
- ALL custom functions must use single object destructuring parameter
- NO console.log â€” use NestJS Logger

## Task: Create AES-256-GCM Encryption Utility

### Step 1: Write failing test

File: `src/unit-tests/encryption.util.unit-spec.ts`

Test cases:
- encrypt + decrypt returns original value
- Two encryptions of same plaintext produce different ciphertexts (random IV)
- Decrypt of garbage input throws

Look at existing unit tests in `src/unit-tests/` for patterns (e.g., `common.util.unit-spec.ts`).

### Step 2: Run test to verify it fails
Run: `npm run test:unit -- --testPathPattern encryption`
Expected: FAIL â€” module not found

### Step 3: Implement encryption utility

File: `src/utils/encryption.util.ts`

- Algorithm: `aes-256-gcm`
- IV: 16 random bytes
- Auth tag: 16 bytes
- Output format: base64url encoded `iv + authTag + encrypted`
- Two functions:
  - `encrypt({ plaintext, key }: { plaintext: string; key: string }): string`
  - `decrypt({ ciphertext, key }: { ciphertext: string; key: string }): string`
- Use `node:crypto` â€” `createCipheriv`, `createDecipheriv`, `randomBytes`
- NO comments in code

### Step 4: Run test to verify it passes
Run: `npm run test:unit -- --testPathPattern encryption`
Expected: PASS

### Step 5: Run Prettier
Run: `npx prettier --write src/utils/encryption.util.ts src/unit-tests/encryption.util.unit-spec.ts`

### Step 6: Commit
```bash
git add src/utils/encryption.util.ts src/unit-tests/encryption.util.unit-spec.ts
git commit -m "feat: add AES-256-GCM encryption utility for OAuth token storage"
```

Report status: DONE, DONE_WITH_CONCERNS, NEEDS_CONTEXT, or BLOCKED

content.text: I'll start by reading the relevant files to understand the conventions and patterns.

content.content:
1â†’# CLAUDE.md
     2â†’
     3â†’This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4â†’
     5â†’## Tools & Requirements
     6â†’
     7â†’- **Node.js**: >=v24.13.0 (see `.node-version`, `.nvmrc`)
     8â†’- **npm**: >=11.0.0
     9â†’- **Core stack**: NestJS v11, Fastify, TypeScript (strict mode)
    10â†’- **Testing**: Jest (unit + e2e)
    11â†’- **Code quality**: ESLint v9+ (flat config), Prettier, Husky
    12â†’
    13â†’## Key Commands
    14â†’
    15â†’```bash
    16â†’# Development
    17â†’npm run start:dev          # Hot reload via webpack
    18â†’npm run start:debug        # Debug mode with hot reload
    19â†’npm run build              # Production build
    20â†’npm run start:prod         # Run production build
    21â†’
    22â†’# Testing
    23â†’npm run test               # Full suite (lint, format, type-check, unit, e2e)
    24â†’npm run test:unit          # Unit tests only
    25â†’npm run test:e2e           # E2E tests only
    26â†’npm run test:coverage      # Full suite with coverage reports
    27â†’npm run test:unit:coverage # Unit tests with coverage
    28â†’npm run test:e2e:coverage  # E2E tests with coverage
    29â†’
    30â†’# Code Quality
    31â†’npm run code-quality-check # lint + format + type-check
    32â†’npm run lint               # Check linting
    33â†’npm run lint:fix           # Auto-fix linting issues
    34â†’npm run format             # Prettier format
    35â†’npm run type-check         # TypeScript check without emit
    36â†’```
    37â†’
    38â†’## Architecture
    39â†’
    40â†’NestJS microservice with Fastify adapter, strict TypeScript, and modular domain organization.
    41â†’
    42â†’### Project Structure
    43â†’
    44â†’```
    45â†’src/
    46â†’â”œâ”€â”€ modules/
    47â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ app/              # Root application module
    48â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ health/           # Health check endpoints (liveness + readiness)
    49â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ env-config/       # Environment configuration & validation
    50â†’â”œâ”€â”€ configs/              # Configuration files
    51â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ env-validations.config.ts  # class-validator decorators for env vars
    52â†’â”œâ”€â”€ constants/            # Constants and enums
    53â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ env-variables.constant.ts  # Env variable name string exports
    54â†’â”œâ”€â”€ dto/                  # Data Transfer Objects
    55â†’â”œâ”€â”€ utils/                # Utility functions
    56â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ common.util.ts           # Environment detection helpers
    57â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ get-env-file.util.ts     # Env file loader & validator
    58â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ get-env-variable.util.ts # getEnv standalone helper
    59â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ setup-swagger.util.ts    # Swagger/OpenAPI setup
    60â†’â”œâ”€â”€ middlewares/          # Custom middlewares
    61â†’â”œâ”€â”€ interfaces/           # TypeScript interfaces
    62â†’â”œâ”€â”€ decorators/           # Custom decorators
    63â†’â”œâ”€â”€ interceptors/         # NestJS interceptors
    64â†’â”œâ”€â”€ guards/               # Auth guards
    65â†’â””â”€â”€ unit-tests/           # Unit tests (*.unit-spec.ts)
    66â†’test/                     # E2E tests (*.e2e-spec.ts)
    67â†’eslint-rules/             # Custom ESLint rules
    68â†’â””â”€â”€ require-object-params.js  # Enforces single object param rule
    69â†’```
    70â†’
    71â†’### Import Aliases (tsconfig.json)
    72â†’
    73â†’```typescript
    74â†’@config/*        â†’ src/configs/*
    75â†’@constants/*     â†’ src/constants/*
    76â†’@modules/*       â†’ src/modules/*
    77â†’@utils/*         â†’ src/utils/*
    78â†’@middlewares/*   â†’ src/middlewares/*
    79â†’@services/*      â†’ src/services/*
    80â†’@interfaces/*    â†’ src/interfaces/*
    81â†’@decorators/*    â†’ src/decorators/*
    82â†’@dto/*           â†’ src/dto/*
    83â†’@guards/*        â†’ src/guards/*
    84â†’@interceptors/*  â†’ src/interceptors/*
    85â†’```
    86â†’
    87â†’## Development Rules
    88â†’
    89â†’### Code Style
    90â†’
    91â†’- **NO COMMENTS**, **NO EXPLANATIONS** in code (except ESLint disable commands)
    92â†’- **ALWAYS INSPECT EXISTING FILES** before creating new ones â€” match exact patterns:
    93â†’  - `.service.ts` â†’ check similar modules
    94â†’  - `.controller.ts` â†’ check `/src/modules/`
    95â†’  - `.dto.ts` â†’ check `/src/dto/` or module folders
    96â†’  - `.util.ts` â†’ check `/src/utils/`
    97â†’  - `.unit-spec.ts` â†’ check `/src/unit-tests/`
    98â†’  - `.e2e-spec.ts` â†’ check `/test/`
    99â†’  - `.interface.ts` â†’ check `/src/interfaces/`
   100â†’- No `console.log` â€” use NestJS `Logger` instead
   101â†’- No relative imports â€” use `@aliases` only
   102â†’- All functions must have explicit return types
   103â†’- All custom functions must use a single object destructuring parameter (enforced by custom ESLint rule `require-object-params`)
   104â†’
   105â†’### Environment Variables (CRITICAL)
   106â†’
   107â†’**ALWAYS follow this complete process when adding new environment variables:**
   108â†’
   109â†’#### Step 1: Add to Environment Files
   110â†’
   111â†’Add to all four env files:
   112â†’
   113â†’```bash
   114â†’# .env, .env.example
   115â†’NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=default_value   # Description of what this variable does
   116â†’```
   117â†’
   118â†’```bash
   119â†’# .env.test, .env.test.example
   120â†’NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=test_value
   121â†’```
   122â†’
   123â†’#### Step 2: Create Constant Export
   124â†’
   125â†’Add to `src/constants/env-variables.constant.ts`:
   126â†’
   127â†’```typescript
   128â†’export const NEW_VARIABLE_NAME = 'NEW_VARIABLE_NAME';
   129â†’```
   130â†’
   131â†’#### Step 3: Add Validation Rule
   132â†’
   133â†’Add to `src/configs/env-validations.config.ts` in the `EnvironmentVariables` class:
   134â†’
   135â†’```typescript
   136â†’@IsString()    // Use appropriate validator
   137â†’@IsNotEmpty()
   138â†’NEW_VARIABLE_NAME: string;
   139â†’```
   140â†’
   141â†’**Common validators:** `@IsString()`, `@IsNumber()`, `@IsBoolean()`, `@IsEnum(EnumType)`, `@IsNotEmpty()`, `@Min()`, `@Max()`, `@IsUrl()`
   142â†’
   143â†’#### Step 4: Add to Environment Loader
   144â†’
   145â†’Add to `src/utils/get-env-file.util.ts` in `getEnvVariables()`:
   146â†’
   147â†’```typescript
   148â†’NEW_VARIABLE_NAME: process.env['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME'],
   149â†’```
   150â†’
   151â†’#### Step 5: Add Test Coverage
   152â†’
   153â†’Add to `test/env-config.module.e2e-spec.ts`:
   154â†’
   155â†’```typescript
   156â†’// In beforeAll hook
   157â†’testEnvVariables['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME'] = String(envConfig['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME']);
   158â†’
   159â†’// Test case
   160â†’it('should load NEW_VARIABLE_NAME correctly', () => {
   161â†’  expect(envConfigService.get<string>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME })).toBe(
   162â†’    testEnvVariables['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME'],
   163â†’  );
   164â†’});
   165â†’```
   166â†’
   167â†’#### Usage: Two Approaches
   168â†’
   169â†’**Approach 1: EnvConfigService (Dependency Injection)**
   170â†’
   171â†’```typescript
   172â†’import { EnvConfigService } from '@modules/env-config/env-config.service';
   173â†’import { NEW_VARIABLE_NAME } from '@constants/env-variables.constant';
   174â†’
   175â†’@Injectable()
   176â†’export class MyService {
   177â†’  constructor(private readonly configService: EnvConfigService) {}
   178â†’
   179â†’  someMethod(): void {
   180â†’    const value = this.configService.get<string>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME });
   181â†’    const numericValue = this.configService.get<number>({
   182â†’      key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME,
   183â†’      converter: Number,
   184â†’    });
   185â†’  }
   186â†’}
   187â†’```
   188â†’
   189â†’**Approach 2: getEnv Utility (Standalone)**
   190â†’
   191â†’```typescript
   192â†’import { getEnv } from '@utils/get-env-variable.util';
   193â†’import { NEW_VARIABLE_NAME } from '@constants/env-variables.constant';
   194â†’
   195â†’const value = getEnv<string>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME });
   196â†’const numericValue = getEnv<number>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME, converter: Number });
   197â†’```
   198â†’
   199â†’**Important:**
   200â†’- **ALWAYS** complete ALL 5 steps â€” missing any step causes validation failures
   201â†’- **NEVER** access `process.env` directly in application code
   202â†’- **ALWAYS** use constants from `@constants/env-variables.constant`
   203â†’
   204â†’### Logging
   205â†’
   206â†’```typescript
   207â†’import { Logger } from '@nestjs/common';
   208â†’
   209â†’export class ClassName {
   210â†’  private readonly logger = new Logger(ClassName.name);
   211â†’}
   212â†’```
   213â†’
   214â†’### Common Patterns
   215â†’
   216â†’```typescript
   217â†’// Swagger decorators on controllers
   218â†’@ApiOperation({ summary: 'Description' })
   219â†’@ApiResponse({ status: HttpStatus.OK, description: 'Success' })
   220â†’```
   221â†’
   222â†’## ESLint Configuration
   223â†’
   224â†’- **No relative imports** (`no-restricted-imports` with `.*` pattern)
   225â†’- **No console** â€” use Logger
   226â†’- **Explicit return types** on all functions (`@typescript-eslint/explicit-module-boundary-types`)
   227â†’- **Import order**: builtin â†’ external â†’ internal (`@aliases`) â†’ type, alphabetized, newline between groups
   228â†’- **Custom rule** `require-object-params`: all custom functions must use a single object destructuring parameter
   229â†’- **Relaxed rules** in `*.spec.ts`, `test/**`, `src/decorators/**`, `src/middlewares/**`, `src/interceptors/**`, `src/guards/**`
   230â†’
   231â†’## TypeScript Configuration
   232â†’
   233â†’- Target ES2023, strict mode with all checks enabled
   234â†’- `strictPropertyInitialization: false` (NestJS DI pattern)
   235â†’- `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature` enabled â€” be precise with types
   236â†’- Incremental compilation enabled for faster builds
   237â†’- Separate `tsconfig.test.json` relaxes strict checks for test files
   238â†’
   239â†’## Testing
   240â†’
   241â†’- **Unit**: `*.unit-spec.ts` in `src/unit-tests/`
   242â†’- **E2E**: `*.e2e-spec.ts` in `test/`
   243â†’- **Coverage**: Separate reports in `coverage-unit/` and `coverage-e2e/`
   244â†’- Test files use `tsconfig.test.json` which relaxes strict checks
   245â†’- **95% minimum coverage** across branches, functions, lines, statements
   246â†’
   247â†’### Jest Config Files
   248â†’
   249â†’```
   250â†’test/
   251â†’â”œâ”€â”€ jest.config.ts              # Shared base (moduleNameMapper, transform, preset)
   252â†’â”œâ”€â”€ jest.coverage.config.ts     # Base + 95% thresholds
   253â†’â”œâ”€â”€ jest-unit.config.ts         # Unit runner (testRegex: unit-spec.ts)
   254â†’â”œâ”€â”€ jest-unit.coverage.config.ts # Unit + coverage enforcement
   255â†’â”œâ”€â”€ jest-e2e.config.ts          # E2E runner (testRegex: e2e-spec.ts)
   256â†’â””â”€â”€ jest-e2e.coverage.config.ts  # E2E + coverage enforcement (controllers + services only)
   257â†’```
   258â†’
   259â†’### Unit Test Patterns
   260â†’
   261â†’```typescript
   262â†’// Service test â€” mock dependencies via Test.createTestingModule
   263â†’const module: TestingModule = await Test.createTestingModule({
   264â†’  providers: [
   265â†’    MyService,
   266â†’    { provide: MyDependency, useValue: mockDependency },
   267â†’  ],
   268â†’}).compile();
   269â†’service = module.get<MyService>(MyService);
   270â†’
   271â†’// Spy on Logger to prevent console noise and assert calls
   272â†’loggerSpy = jest.spyOn(Logger.prototype, 'log').mockImplementation(jest.fn());
   273â†’
   274â†’// Always clear mocks between tests
   275â†’afterEach(() => jest.clearAllMocks());
   276â†’```
   277â†’
   278â†’### E2E Test Patterns
   279â†’
   280â†’```typescript
   281â†’// HTTP testing â€” let TerminusModule run the real health check chain
   282â†’const moduleFixture = await Test.createTestingModule({
   283â†’  imports: [TerminusModule.forRoot({ gracefulShutdownTimeoutMs: 0, errorLogStyle: 'json' })],
   284â†’  controllers: [MyController],
   285â†’  providers: [MyService, { provide: Dependency, useValue: mock }],
   286â†’}).compile();
   287â†’
   288â†’const app = moduleFixture.createNestApplication();
   289â†’const httpServer = app.getHttpServer() as App;
   290â†’await app.init();
   291â†’
   292â†’// Use supertest for HTTP assertions
   293â†’await request(httpServer).get('/endpoint').expect(200);
   294â†’```
   295â†’
   296â†’### Coverage Requirements
   297â†’
   298â†’- **95% minimum** on branches, functions, lines, statements â€” enforced by Jest, CI fails if not met
   299â†’- Unit coverage collects from all `src/**/*.ts` (excluding `main.ts`, modules, interfaces, constants, test files)
   300â†’- E2E coverage collects only from `*.(controller|service).ts` files
   301â†’
   302â†’## Key Dependencies
   303â†’
   304â†’### Core
   305â†’
   306â†’- **@nestjs/\*** â€” NestJS framework and modules
   307â†’- **@nestjs/platform-fastify** â€” Fastify adapter for performance
   308â†’- **@nestjs/terminus** â€” Health check module
   309â†’- **@nestjs/swagger** â€” OpenAPI/Swagger documentation
   310â†’
   311â†’### Validation & Transform
   312â†’
   313â†’- **class-validator** + **class-transformer** â€” DTO validation and transformation
   314â†’
   315â†’### Utilities
   316â†’
   317â†’- **rxjs** â€” Reactive patterns
   318â†’- **axios** / **@nestjs/axios** â€” HTTP requests
   319â†’

content.text:
Status: **DONE**

All steps completed successfully:

1. **Test file** written at `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend/src/unit-tests/encryption.util.unit-spec.ts` â€” 4 test cases covering encrypt/decrypt roundtrip, random IV (different ciphertexts for same plaintext), and garbage input throws.

2. **Failing test confirmed** â€” module not found as expected.

3. **Implementation** written at `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend/src/utils/encryption.util.ts` â€” uses `aes-256-gcm`, 16-byte random IV, 16-byte auth tag, output format `base64url(iv + authTag + encrypted)`.

4. **All 4 tests pass**.

5. **Prettier** ran â€” both files unchanged (already compliant).

6. **Committed** as `feat: add AES-256-GCM encryption utility for OAuth token storage` (commit `16d1fb4`).
